ref: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
text: Ka Yesu 'yɛr, “Yaa kar a be a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na maal ba a pielfʊ daar ‘Maal daba nɩ pɔɔ’ ”, (A Pielfʊ Gãn
translated: Jesus said, "Why have you forsaken me?" (Matthew 27:46)

ref: Jesus heard them and answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
text: Yesu wõn a lɛ ban 'yɛr, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Nɩrɛ na kpɛ̃mɛ ba chiine tɩtɩlɛ jie ɛ, tɩchɛ baalaa lɛ ma chen.
translated: Jesus spoke to them in parables, saying, "A man planted his seeds on the path, and the birds came and ate them."

ref: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
text: Ka a wʊ poturbo 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Nyɛ lɛ sɩn nyɛ paanʊ̃ɔ a dãlpʊɔ nyã pɔ tʊ̃ɔ kãan a nɩyɔɔ banyãna?”
translated: He said to the thief, "What did you eat that you gave to the thief when you came to my house where I was sleeping?"

ref: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
text: Nɩyɔɔ wan wa duo na ba sɛɛ a die wa tɩ yin a yõo. Ka wa 'yɛrɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr kʊrɔ ba.
translated: Niyoo went with them to the place where they eat. And when they arrived at Naangmin's eating place, they ate.

ref: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
text: A poturbo wa 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Fʊ bɔ̃ɔn naa an kpɛ a Faraseemɩne ban wõ a 'yɛr nyã fʊn 'yɛr ɔ?”
translated: If you see and understand what the Pharisees are saying, what does it mean?

ref: Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
text: Bojũu wʊ bɔ̃ɔn naa ɩka a nyuur ban tara nɩ a Yesu lɛ so ban nyɔɔ wʊ waan a wʊ sɛ̃.
translated: Bojũu went to the good place and taught them the word of Jesus, so they heard and wanted to follow him.

ref: Isn't he the son of the carpenter? Isn't Mary his mother, and aren't James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?
text: A ba ɩ a mɩmɩɛrɛ bidaba lɛ ɛ? A ba ɩ a wʊ ma lɛ ba bʊɔlɔ a Mɛɛr ɛ? A ba ɩ a wʊ yɛɛr nɩ Jemesɩ nɩ Josefʊ nɩ Simon nɩ Judas ɛ?
translated: Does he have brothers? Is his mother named Mary? Are these his brothers: James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas?

ref: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
text: An piel a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ a Yesu piel 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ ba ɩka wʊn chen Jerusalɛm tɩ di dɔɔyɛ a nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a nɛɛ wiwiilbe nuur pɔ, ka ba kʊ wʊ, ka a ber ata daar a wʊ lɛ ir ɩ vʊr.
translated: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he spoke to the disciples and the Pharisees who were following him along the way, telling them parables.

ref: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
text: Ka a nɩbɛ bamɩne na be be bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba gar a Yesu, be lɛ ba tɔɔ gbʊr kaara wʊ. Ka wʊn sãa naa a daba nũu baal a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir bɩɩ.
translated: And he went to the place with the words, and he called him Jesus, and he gave the child the name Jesus. And when he came, he fulfilled the word of the Lord, as the Holy Spirit had spoken.

ref: Jesus told his disciples not to tell anyone he was the Messiah. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Mark
text: Ka wʊ pãa kpãkpãan a wʊ poturbo ɩka ba taa 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ nɩrɛ jaa ɩka 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Nɩ-iraa ɛ.
translated: The children were playing when the potter came on Thursday and Friday to Nira.

ref: Again Jesus went to pray and said, “My Father, if there is no other way, and I must suffer, I will still do what you want.”
text: Wʊ lɛ chen a gbɛɛ ayi sʊɔ tɩ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn. “N Sãa, ala ɩ a dɔɔyɛ ŋmele nyã kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ po ma ɛ, ka maa ba nyũn wʊ ɛ, fʊ̃ʊ bɔfʊ wʊ ɩ.”
translated: He went to Chen's house and saw Nãaŋmɩn there. "O Saa, why did you do this thing to me? You have caused me to suffer, you have wronged me."

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: Ba wa naa a Yesu sɛ̃ wa nyɛ a daba 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ tara a sɩdɛyɔɔ gele ka wʊ chɩ su bonsɩ jãnɛ, ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ ba.
translated: He went and Jesus said to them, "When you go to speak to many people to heal them, then they went to another village and he taught them."

ref: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
text: A mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ na wa ara gar ju sãlsɔɔ, a wɛr wʊ jaa sɔb naa tɩ tãn a mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ balfʊ sɔ̃ɔ.
translated: They went to the farm to plant yam, and then they stayed there to eat food and returned home to sleep.

ref: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
text: “Yɩ ta sɔɔ ka ba bʊɔlɔ yɩ, Wiwiilbe ɛ, nɩkpɛ̃ɛ bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ lɛ yɩ tara. Yɩ jaa ɩn yɛɛr.
translated: You eat the food where you are, Wiwiilbe, the things that are said there, you come. You go there.

ref: Don't you think you should show pity to someone else, as I did to you?”
text: Fʊ mɩ kʊ̃ naa kaa a fʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃tɔ sʊɔ nyã nɩbaal nɩtãa a lɛ ɩn kaa a fʊ nɩbaal ɛ?’
translated: Why did you do this here now and not that?

ref: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. He becomes like one person with his wife.
text: na wʊ 'yɛr, “A lɛ jũu, daba na bar a wʊ sãa nɩ a wʊ ma, tɩchɛ ka 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ pɔɔ ɩ bʊ̃'yen, ka ba jaa ayi ɩ ãgãn bʊ̃'yen? (A Pielfʊ Gãn
translated: In the beginning, when the Lord created the heavens and the earth, which did not yet exist, and there was no rain upon the earth, and there was no one to till the soil of the earth? (Genesis)

ref: As Jesus was leaving that place, two blind men began following him and shouting, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Yesu na wa yi a be chiine, jɔ̃n ayi turo wʊ naa, na ba gboolo bʊɔlɔ, “Kaa sɩ nɩbaal, David Bie.”
translated: Jesus and he said to him, John taught you all, and they did works, "Come see my Lord, David Bie."

ref: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
text: Ka a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ bʊɔl wʊ wa sʊʊr wʊ, ‘Fʊ̃ʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃putuo sʊɔ. Fʊn sʊɔr ma jũu lɛ so ka n vɛ̃ a sãn bar fʊ.
translated: Then he went to the river to drink water, he drank it all. Then they came there to eat food with him.

ref: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na turo a Nãaŋmɩn sɔr, 'lɔ lɛ a n yɛbɛ nɩ a n yɛpɔɔ nɩ a n ma.”
translated: If you go to the path of Nãaŋmɩn, you will not find it and you will not reach it.

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: A Nãa Hɛrɔd na wa bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a yɛ̃ dẽme banyã na yɩ a sapar bɛl wʊ naa a suur kpɛ wʊ a wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ sojasɩ ɩka ba chen tɩ kʊ dabile jaa na be a Bɛtɩlɛhɛm na ta yuom ayi bɩɩ na ba ta sɛrɛ ɛ, yi a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ jaa wʊn sʊʊr bɔ̃ɔn yi a yɛ̃ bɔ̃ɔnbɔ sɛ̃.
translated: When King Herod heard this, he began to search carefully to know where the child who was born would be found, and he sent them to Bethlehem, saying, "Go and inquire diligently about the child, and when you have found him, report to me so that I may come and worship him too."

ref: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
text: Ban wa chen baar, Yesu piel 'yɛrɛ a Jɔɔn 'yɛr kʊrɔ a nɩyɔɔ, “Bo lɛ yɩ dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ ɩka yɩ tɩ kaa? A jɩmur a sɛsɛbɛ na dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ bɩɩ?
translated: There was Ban; Jesus said to John, "What should you do for the people so that your name may be known? Is he the one who sins with the people?"

ref: But don't act like them. If you want to be great, you must be the servant of all the others.
text: A kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ be a lɛ a yɩ pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ 'lɔ na bʊɔrɔ ɩka wʊ ɩ a yɩ kpɛ̃ɛ, wʊ ɩ a yɩ tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃.
translated: He went to the house to look for it, and when he found it, he took it and came back, and he sat down.

ref: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
text: “Wiwile, A Mosesɩ nɛbinsɩ a jaa pɔ, buor lɛ ɩ kpɛ̃ɛ?”
translated: Moses, why have you left us?

ref: But while everyone was sleeping, an enemy came and scattered weed seeds in the field and then left.
text: nɩrɛ jaa na wa gã gur baar, a dɔ̃sʊɔ wan wa bʊr mɔɩ 'yɔ̃ɔ a chi pɔ tɩ na wʊ kul.
translated: He sees the big houses, and he lives in a town there, going to the farm.

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they have has been offered to God.
text: Tɩchɛ yɩn 'yɛr ɩka nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na 'yɛr a wʊ sãa bɩɩ ma ɩka, sʊ̃ʊfʊ 'lɔ jaa ɩn tara naa kʊ fʊ, n den kʊ Nãaŋmɩn.
translated: The one who took his child to the farm and did not return, the child died, and he then called upon Nãaŋmɩn.
